Title: SeriesTimeline Continuity & AI Deep Features
Description: Deliver two capabilities that mature ScriptOS from a production tool into an intelligent series platform: (1) the Continuity Service with the SeriesTimeline graph — cross-episode story-day chronology ingesting continuity assertions from on-set sync, automatic violation detection (wardrobe, injuries, props, relationships), AI-assisted ambiguous case resolution, and a full violation management workflow; and (2) AI Deep Features — character voice registry completion with few-shot reference scenes, semantic cache (pgvector ANN at 0.97 threshold), advanced analytical features (consistency_check, coverage_analysis, structure_analysis), C2PA manifest embedding in PDF exports, and hybrid search hardening with BM25/vector weight tuning. This epic ends with the Beta milestone (Sprint 11 end, Week 24): 3 production teams using breakdown + scheduling + on-set module. Reference: Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprints 11 and 12, Wiki/05-ai-governance.md, Wiki/06-offline-sync.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- On-set continuity assertion → syncs to cloud → appears in SeriesTimeline → violation detected if contradictory
- Cross-episode violation: character injury in episode 3 flagged as inconsistent with healthy appearance in episode 4 (story day 6)
- Beta milestone (Sprint 11 end, Week 24): 3 production teams using breakdown + scheduling + on-set
- Continuity Service deployed to staging
- Second dialogue suggestion for same context hits semantic cache (ledger entry model_id = 'semantic_cache')
- Voice violation warning shown when AI suggests a character's forbidden word
- Coverage analysis report generated for a 90-page test script
- Hybrid search tuned: Recall@10 improved ≥ 15% vs pre-tuning baseline on eval set
Tags: continuity; series-timeline; ai-deep; voice-registry; semantic-cache; beta; sprint-11; sprint-12

Title: Continuity Service — SeriesTimeline and Cross-Episode Violation Detection
Description: Build the Continuity Service to maintain the SeriesTimeline graph: story days across all episodes with assertions (wardrobe, injuries, props, VFX, relationships) attached to story days and time-of-day slots. Ingest assertions from supervisor.assertion.synced NATS events. Detect violations by contradiction rules and AI-assisted checks for ambiguous cases. Surface violations with affected episode context. Violation resolution workflow (mark as resolved/intentional/error). Reference: Wiki/03-data-model.md §4, Wiki/06-offline-sync.md, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 11.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Story days created and linked across episodes; assertions ingested within 10s of NATS event
- Violation detection: contradictory assertion on same subject + story day creates violation record
- Cross-episode violation shows impact on downstream episodes
- AI-assisted check resolves ambiguous violations where rule-based detection is inconclusive
- Continuity Service GraphQL subgraph federated into Apollo Router
- Beta milestone: 3 production teams onboarded with continuity module access

Title: Character Voice Registry and Few-Shot AI Dialogue

Enhance AI dialogue suggestions to enforce voice profiles (forbidden words, vocabulary level, speech patterns). Voice violation warnings shown inline in suggestion panel. Reference: Wiki/05-ai-governance.md, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 12.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Reference scenes selectable per character; stored as voiceProfile.referenceSceneIds
- AI context assembler uses reference scene dialogue as few-shot examples in cacheable prefix
- Voice violation warning shown when AI suggestion contains forbidden word for target character
- Voice profile editor fully functional in Bible panel: vocabularyLevel, speechPatterns, forbiddenWords

Title: AI Semantic Cache and Advanced Analytics
Description: Implement the AI semantic cache using pgvector ANN search (0.97 threshold) with TTL (24h creative, 7d analytical). Deliver advanced AI features: consistency_check (non-generative, Tier 1), coverage_analysis (Tier 3 full script report), structure_analysis (act breaks, pacing). C2PA manifest embedding in PDF exports. WGA compliance report export. Reference: Wiki/05-ai-governance.md, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 12.

- Semantic cache: second identical dialogue request returns cache hit; ledger entry model_id = 'semantic_cache', cost_usd = 0

Title: Hybrid Search Hardening and Faceted Filters
Description: Tune Elasticsearch BM25 parameters (b, k1) and vector-to-BM25 weight ratio in RRF. Validate on a human-evaluated test set. Add faceted search (filter by episode, scene type, character name). Measure and assert Recall@10 improvement of ≥ 15% vs pre-tuning baseline. Reference: Wiki/10-search.md, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 12.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Recall@10 improved ≥ 15% on eval set after BM25 + RRF tuning
- Faceted search: character filter returns only scenes containing that character
- Facet aggregations returned alongside search results for filter UI
- Search GraphQL updated with facet input types and aggregation output

Title: As a continuity supervisor I can track cross-episode continuity assertions on a story day timeline and see violations automatically detected
Description: Cross-episode continuity tracking is one of the most labour-intensive tasks in multi-episode production. The SeriesTimeline provides a single source of truth for what happened on each story day across all episodes. Violations surface automatically so the continuity supervisor doesn't have to manually compare episode notes. Reference: Wiki/03-data-model.md §4.
Acceptance Criteria:
- SeriesTimeline view shows story days as horizontal timeline; each day lists assertions grouped by subject
- Continuity assertions from on-set Tauri app sync to cloud and appear in SeriesTimeline within 10s
- Violation detected when two assertions on the same subject and story day are contradictory (e.g. 'wearing blue shirt' vs 'wearing red shirt')
- Cross-episode violation: character injury in episode 3 flagged as inconsistent with healed state in episode 4 (story day 6)
- Violation detail shows both conflicting assertions with source episode, scene, and take
- Violation severity levels: ERROR (must resolve before lock), WARNING (review recommended), INFO (informational)
Parent: Continuity Service — SeriesTimeline and Cross-Episode Violation Detection

Title: As a continuity supervisor I can resolve violations by marking them as resolved intentional or an error so the violation list stays actionable
Description: Without a resolution workflow, the violation list grows indefinitely and becomes noise. Resolved violations should be archived with the resolution reason so decisions are auditable. Intentional violations (e.g. a character's wardrobe inconsistency is deliberate for a dream sequence) are valid and should be preserved as context. Reference: Wiki/03-data-model.md §4.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Violation can be marked as resolved (contradiction corrected at source), intentional (deliberate for creative reason), or error (false positive — rule was wrong)
- Resolution requires a text note explaining the decision
- Resolved/intentional violations move to the 'Archived' tab; ERROR violations cannot be archived without note
- Resolution recorded with actor (user) and timestamp in audit log
- Unresolved ERROR violations block publish-saga policy validation (Sprint 15 full Legal Service integration)
Parent: Continuity Service — SeriesTimeline and Cross-Episode Violation Detection

Title: As a writer AI dialogue suggestions respect my character's voice profile and warn me when the AI suggests vocabulary that violates their established voice
Description: Voice consistency is one of the defining qualities of great television writing. If Walter White suddenly uses millennial slang, the AI has failed the writer. Character voice profiles must gate AI suggestions before they reach the writer. Warnings must be specific ('WALTER would not say literally') not generic. Reference: Wiki/05-ai-governance.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Voice profile editor fully functional: vocabularyLevel (BASIC/CONVERSATIONAL/SOPHISTICATED/TECHNICAL), speechPatterns (tag list), forbiddenWords (red chip list), referenceSceneIds (scene picker)
- requestDialogueSuggestion: character guard checks suggestion text against forbiddenWords before returning
- Voice violation: panel shows amber warning 'Flagged: [CHARACTER] would not use this vocabulary' with the specific forbidden word highlighted
- Reference scenes injected as few-shot examples in AI context; dialogue suggestions measurably match character's established patterns
Parent: Character Voice Registry and Few-Shot AI Dialogue

Title: As a writer repeated AI queries for the same context are served from the semantic cache instantly at zero cost so I can iterate rapidly on dialogue
Description: Waiting 3-5s per AI suggestion interrupts creative flow. The semantic cache avoids redundant API calls for similar queries. Writers often rephrase the same request slightly — the 0.97 embedding similarity threshold catches semantically identical queries. Cache hits are free and instant. Reference: Wiki/05-ai-governance.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Second dialogue suggestion for same scene and character context returns from cache in < 200ms
- Cache hit visible in AI ledger: model_id = 'semantic_cache', cost_usd = 0
- Cache miss on significantly different query (different character or different scene) — does not hit stale cache
- Cache TTL respected: 24h for dialogue_suggestion; expired entries not returned (re-fetches from model)
- Cache reduces AI costs by ≥ 30% in a simulated 10-writer alpha session
Parent: AI Semantic Cache and Advanced Analytics

Title: Create Atlas DDL for series_timeline.timeline_story_days story_day_scenes continuity_assertions and assertion_violations tables
Description: In packages/db/schema/series_timeline.hcl: (1) series_timeline.timeline_story_days: id UUID PK, project_id UUID FK, story_day_number INT, calendar_date DATE, episode_id UUID (nullable for multi-ep days), notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. (2) series_timeline.story_day_scenes: id UUID PK, story_day_id UUID FK, scene_id UUID, episode_id UUID, time_of_day ENUM(DAWN,MORNING,AFTERNOON,EVENING,NIGHT,CONTINUOUS). (3) series_timeline.continuity_assertions: id UUID PK, story_day_id UUID FK, subject TEXT NOT NULL, assertion_text TEXT NOT NULL, assertion_type ENUM(wardrobe,injury,prop,relationship,vfx,location), confidence FLOAT4, photo_s3_key TEXT, source_episode_id UUID, source_scene_id UUID, source_take_id UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_synced_from_onset BOOL DEFAULT false. (4) series_timeline.assertion_violations: id UUID PK, assertion_a_id UUID FK, assertion_b_id UUID FK, violation_type TEXT, severity ENUM(ERROR,WARNING,INFO), status ENUM(open,resolved,intentional,error), resolution_note TEXT, resolved_by UUID, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Indexes: (project_id, story_day_number) on story_days; (story_day_id, subject, assertion_type) on assertions. Apply.
Parent: As a continuity supervisor I can track cross-episode continuity assertions on a story day timeline

Title: Build Continuity Service core with story day CRUD assertion ingestion and NATS consumer for supervisor.assertion.synced events
Description: In services/continuity/src/: (1) StoryDayService: createStoryDay, linkSceneToDay (upsert story_day_scenes), getStoryDayAssertions gRPC methods. (2) AssertionService: createAssertion (manual), getAssertionsBySubject, getAssertionsByStoryDay gRPC methods. (3) NATS JetStream push consumer on supervisor.assertion.synced (durable: continuity-ingestor): parse { assertionId, sceneId, storyDayRef, subject, assertionText, assertionType, photoS3Key }; upsert into series_timeline.continuity_assertions with is_synced_from_onset = true; run ViolationDetector.checkForViolations(newAssertion); publish continuity.violation.detected { violationId } if violations found. SLA: assertions processed within 10s of NATS event. Integration test: publish supervisor.assertion.synced; assert assertion in DB within 12s.
Parent: As a continuity supervisor I can track cross-episode continuity assertions on a story day timeline

Title: Implement violation detection engine with rule-based contradiction detection and AI-assisted resolution for ambiguous cases
Description: In services/continuity/src/violation/detector.ts implement ViolationDetector.checkForViolations(newAssertion). Rule-based detection: (1) Query all existing assertions for same project, same subject, same story_day_id. (2) For same assertion_type: apply type-specific rules. Wardrobe: identical subject on same story day with different assertion_text → conflict. Injury: if prior assertion says 'injured' and new says 'uninjured' on a later story_day_number → potential violation. (3) Cross-episode: if story_day_number matches across episodes, compare assertions. (4) Confidence threshold: rule-based detection only fires when contradictions are unambiguous (e.g. 'blue shirt' vs 'red shirt'); create violation with severity=ERROR. Ambiguous cases (e.g. 'appears tired' vs 'appears rested'): call AIService.checkConsistency(assertionA, assertionB, context) → if AI returns conflict probability > 0.7: create violation with severity=WARNING. Write violations to series_timeline.assertion_violations. Integration test: insert two contradictory wardrobe assertions; assert violation created with severity=ERROR.
Parent: As a continuity supervisor I can track cross-episode continuity assertions on a story day timeline

Title: Build Continuity Service GraphQL subgraph with StoryDay ContinuityAssertion AssertionViolation types and violation resolution mutation
Description: In services/continuity/src/graphql/schema.graphql: type StoryDay { id, projectId, storyDayNumber, calendarDate, scenes: [Scene!]!, assertions: [ContinuityAssertion!]!, violations: [AssertionViolation!]! }. type ContinuityAssertion { id, subject, assertionText, assertionType, confidence, photoS3Key, sourceEpisode, sourceScene, createdAt, isSyncedFromOnset }. type AssertionViolation { id, assertionA, assertionB, violationType, severity, status, resolutionNote, resolvedBy, resolvedAt }. Queries: seriesTimeline(projectId): [StoryDay!]!; storyDay(id): StoryDay!; openViolations(projectId): [AssertionViolation!]!. Mutations: resolveViolation(violationId, status: 'resolved'|'intentional'|'error', note: String!): AssertionViolation!; createManualAssertion(storyDayId, subject, assertionText, assertionType): ContinuityAssertion!. Register subgraph in supergraph.yaml.
Parent: As a continuity supervisor I can track cross-episode continuity assertions on a story day timeline

Title: Complete character voice profile reference scene management in Bible Service and update AI context assembler with few-shot dialogue injection
Description: In services/bible/src/grpc/bible.service.ts implement: (1) SetVoiceProfileReferenceScenes(req: { characterId, referenceSceneIds: UUID[] }): validates max 5 scenes; stores in character_voice_profiles.reference_scene_ids array. (2) GetVoiceProfileWithExamples(req: { characterId }): returns voice profile + fetches scene snapshots for each referenceSceneId from Script Service → extracts dialogue blocks for that character → returns { voiceProfile, dialogueExamples: { sceneRef, lines: string[] }[] }. Title: Implement AI semantic cache using pgvector ANN search with 0.97 similarity threshold and 24h/7d TTL rules
Description: In services/ai/src/cache/semantic-cache.ts implement SemanticCache. Setup: ensure pgvector extension installed; ai.semantic_cache table from Atlas DDL (task 180) with VECTOR(3072) column and IVFFlat index. Methods: (1) lookup(promptText: string, featureType: string): Promise<{ hit: boolean, cachedResponse?: string, cacheId?: UUID }>. Steps: embed promptText using openai.embeddings.create(text-embedding-3-large); run ANN search: SELECT id, cached_response, 1 - (embedding <=> $1) AS similarity FROM ai.semantic_cache WHERE feature_type = $2 AND expires_at > NOW() ORDER BY embedding <=> $1 LIMIT 1. If similarity ≥ 0.97: return cache hit. (2) store(promptText, response, featureType): embed promptText; insert row. TTL: feature_type IN ('dialogue_suggestion') → expires_at = NOW() + INTERVAL '24 hours'; analytical features → 7 days. Integrate into requestDialogueSuggestion: check cache before model call; if hit, write ledger entry with model_id = 'semantic_cache', cost_usd = 0.0. Integration test: store and lookup same prompt; assert similarity = 1.0 and cache hit.
Parent: As a writer repeated AI queries for the same context are served from the semantic cache instantly at zero cost

Title: Implement coverage_analysis and structure_analysis AI features and C2PA manifest embedding in PDF exports
Description: In services/ai/src/features/coverage-analysis.ts implement analyseCoverage({ projectId, scriptId }): Promise<CoverageReport>. Feature tier: Tier 3 (Studio: Opus, Professional: Sonnet). Assembles full script context. Prompt instructs model to return JSON: { logline, synopsis, structureAssessment: { actBreaks: { act, sceneNumber }[], pacingNotes: string, recommend ation: 'PASS'|'CONSIDER'|'PASS_WITH_NOTES' }, characterArcs: { characterName, arcSummary, developmentScore: 1-5 }[], strengths: string[], concerns: string[], coverageNote: string }. In services/ai/src/features/structure-analysis.ts implement analyseStructure with shorter prompt focused on act breaks, pacing, midpoint. C2PA manifest: in Script Service PDF export pipeline, after PDF generation, inject a C2PA manifest JSON block into PDF metadata: { assertions: [{ label: 'com.scriptos.ai_involvement', data: { involvedFeatures: string[], ledgerProjectUrl: string } }] }. Use pdf-lib to write metadata. Integration test: run coverage_analysis on 10-scene test script; assert JSON fields present; assert PDF export contains C2PA metadata.
Parent: As a writer repeated AI queries for the same context are served from the semantic cache instantly at zero cost

Title: Tune Elasticsearch BM25 parameters and RRF weights and add faceted search with episode scene-type and character filters
Description: In services/search/src/tuning/: create a test evaluation set of 30 (query, relevantSceneId) pairs from a reference script. Implement measureRecall(k=10): run hybrid search; compute Recall@10. Baseline: record Recall@10 before tuning. Tuning: (1) BM25: test b in {0.5, 0.75, 1.0} and k1 in {1.0, 1.2, 1.5, 2.0}; pick best Recall@10 combination. (2) RRF: test vector weight via knn num_candidates in {30, 50, 100}. Assert tuned Recall@10 ≥ 15% better than baseline. Faceted search: add bool.filter support in hybridSearch for { episodeIds, sceneTypes, characterNames }. Add aggregations to ES query: { episodes: { terms: { field: 'episode_id' } }, sceneTypes: { terms: { field: 'scene_type' } }, characters: { terms: { field: 'character_names' } } }. Return aggregation results as SearchFacets alongside results. Update Search GraphQL subgraph to return facets. Assert facet counts are correct for a 5-episode project.
Parent: As a writer repeated AI queries for the same context are served from the semantic cache instantly at zero cost

Title: Build SeriesTimeline view continuity violations panel and violation resolution workflow UI in web app
Description: Create apps/web/src/continuity/components/SeriesTimeline.tsx. Data from useSeriesTimelineQuery(projectId). Layout: horizontal scrollable timeline; each StoryDay as a column (width 180px) with day number, calendar date, episode tag, and assertion count badge. Within each story day: assertion chips grouped by type (wardrobe, injury, prop); click chip → AssertionDetail popover showing subject, text, source scene link, photo thumbnail. Violation badge on story day column if violations exist (red for ERROR, amber for WARNING). Create ViolationsPanel.tsx: filterable list of open violations; each row shows violation type, severity badge, conflicting assertions summary, affected episodes, and Resolve dropdown. ResolveViolationModal: select resolution status (Resolved/Intentional/Error) + required note text + submit. Apollo mutation resolveViolation. Vitest test: render SeriesTimeline with 3 story days; 1 day has 2 assertions and 1 violation; assert violation badge rendered; click Resolve; assert resolveViolation mutation called.
Parent: As a continuity supervisor I can track cross-episode continuity assertions on a story day timeline
